question_title: Will a Tucker Carlson-founded political party file FEC Form 1 before September 2026?
summary: Tucker Carlson's early-July 2026 statement that he'll "help build a third party" is purely aspirational — no party name, co-founders, platform, timeline, or funding vehicle. A direct FEC/OpenFEC database check found no Carlson-linked committee as of July 11, and ~7 of the ~8-week window has minimal time-value given zero organizational progress. The fine print further requires Carlson be specifically named as founder/co-founder. Status quo strongly favors NO; small upside from his profile, resources, and the low mechanical bar to filing a Form 1.

factors.description: No FEC Form 1 filed as of July 11; direct FEC/OpenFEC database check found no Carlson-linked committee
Effort is purely aspirational: no party name, co-founders, platform, timeline, or funding vehicle
Carlson framed it as 'help build a third party' and won't run himself — weak personal commitment to being a named founder
Fine print requires Carlson specifically named as founder/co-founder — narrows qualifying events
High profile, resources, stated seriousness, and low mechanical bar to filing could accelerate within the ~7-week window

reasoning:
**Forecast: 5% (NO)**

Tucker Carlson's statement is aspirational talk with no concrete organizational steps — no party name, co-founders, platform, or timeline — and a direct FEC database check found no Carlson-linked committee. The resolution window (July 3–Sept 1) is mostly elapsed with zero progress, and the fine print's requirement that Carlson be named a founder/co-founder further narrows qualifying events. Small upside remains given his profile, resources, and the trivially low bar to filing a Form 1.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after July 3, 2026 and before September 1, 2026, a Form 1 "Statement of Organization" is filed with the US [Federal Election Commission](https://www.fec.gov/data/filings/?data_type=processed) for any political party founded or co-founded by Tucker Carlson.
fine_print:
If there are any ambiguities over whether a Form 1 filing has occurred (such as scenarios in which the authenticity of a filing is in doubt), Metaculus may refer to [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) to verify the truth of the matter. 

Form 1 filings only resolve this question if Carlson is a founder or co-founder of the party, according to credible sources. Thus, edge cases such as a party being officially formed without Carlson being reported as a founder will not count, even if he subsequently endorses or supports the party.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/44414) which opened on 2026-07-03 15:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.

factors.description: No FEC Form 1 filed as of July 11; direct FEC/OpenFEC database check found no Carlson-linked committee
Effort is purely aspirational: no party name, no co-founders, no platform, no timeline, no funding vehicle
Carlson framed it as 'help build a third party' and won't run himself — weak personal commitment to being a named founder
Fine print requires Carlson specifically named as founder/co-founder — narrows qualifying events
High profile, resources, and stated seriousness could accelerate; ~7 weeks remain in window

input.anchor: Base rate for a public figure's aspirational third-party announcement converting to a formal FEC committee filing (with them named as founder) within ~8 weeks: ~10%
input.assessment: Third-party announcements rarely convert to FEC Form 1 filings (with the announcer named as founder) within ~2 months. Carlson's statement is vague aspirational talk. Status quo strongly favors NO. Small upside for his profile/resources and the strict window.
input.calibration_notes: Status quo = NO. Evidence is speculative ('help build'), not concrete (no dates, no co-founders). Not near 50% — committing to low.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether Carlson or an allied operative rapidly files a committee naming him as co-founder. A sudden formal launch would flip this.
input.tool_audit: research() sub-agent returned a thorough answer including a direct FEC/OpenFEC database check — high value. No failures.
input.process_reflection: Straightforward low-base-rate definitional/predictive question. The research tool's direct FEC check was exactly what was needed. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
All five factors are grounded in the research trace. Factor 1 (no FEC filing as of July 11) is verified by two independent checks in the trace: the FEC public committee search (returns no Carlson match) and the OpenFEC API sorted by first_file_date (only the unrelated "Owen Nicholas Carlson" committee, C00928689, filed 2025-11-28). Factor 2 (no party name/co-founders/platform/timeline/funding) matches the NOTUS article fetch verbatim. Factor 3 ("help build a third party," won't run himself) matches multiple sourced quotes. Factor 4 correctly restates the founder/co-founder fine print. Factor 5 is a reasonable, correctly-signed positive factor.

Resolution criteria are provided and engaged (window July 3–Sept 1, founder-naming requirement addressed in Factor 4). Pre-publication check: criteria specify a start date (after July 3) before published_at (July 11); the agent checked through July 11 and found no qualifying filing, so nothing is treated as already-resolved — no problem. Anchor divergence is small (10% → 5%) and justified by ~7 of 8 weeks elapsing with zero organizational progress. The counterargument is genuinely adversarial (cites the Musk "America Party" fast-filing analog, trivial-to-file logic, remaining window) rather than a token gesture. Self-confidence (0.85) is calibrated to the thorough research.

Two minor, non-material notes: (1) The Musk "America Party rapidly produced a filing" claim appears only inside the counterargument, not the trace — acceptable since it's an adversarial device, not a load-bearing factor. (2) The nested research sub-agent hit two asknews 403 errors (lines 140, 206) not reflected in the parent session's 0-error metric; the parent-level "no failures" audit claim is consistent with parent-level ground truth.

My independent estimate lands in the 4–8% range (I'd lean ~6%), consistent with the agent's 5%. No hallucination, double-counting, wrong-direction factors, or resolution misalignment found.
